summary: Remastered soundboard of the legendary Aug 27 1972 show delivers powerful Phil bass, soaring Birdsong and a cosmic Dark Star, though heavy reverb/processing splits opinion on the recording.
review: The performance is widely praised – Jerry’s guitar, Keith’s keys and Phil’s thunderous bass drive a tight, bouncy set highlighted by Dark Star > El Paso, Birdsong, Jack Straw, Promised Land and Playin’ In The Band. The Bertha remaster offers excellent clarity on instruments but its heavy EQ, compression and added reverb give some listeners a synthetic vocal feel and a “studio‑processed” ambience.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers award 5‑star ratings and rave about the show’s energy and standout songs; recording quality is generally good but a minority criticize heavy reverb, pulling the overall score slightly below perfect.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Remaster features strong instrument clarity and solid bass levels, but includes extensive EQ/compression and added reverb that many describe as overly processed or synthetic, especially on vocals.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set is described as tight and bouncy, with seamless transitions that keep the band’s momentum high throughout; fans note excellent pacing and energy from start to finish.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for flawless guitar work, “painting fractals” on Birdsong and almost never flubbing a note.
band_member_comments.Phil: Repeatedly highlighted for powerful, thunderous bass that drives the show.
band_member_comments.Bob: Vocals described as synthetic or fuzzy in this mix; however his banter and occasional vocal moments receive positive mentions.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard playing is noted as especially into it, adding depth to the performance.
